title: Two-parser-style design (char vs byte) is correct and should be defended
description: The codebase has two JSON parsing styles: char-based (Peekable<Chars>) for extract_field, byte-based (positional index returning &[u8] slices) for json_navigate/extract_path. A future contributor will see this as needless duplication and suggest unifying to one parser. Do not do that. The byte-level parser is the only way to return zero-copy &[u8] sub-slices of the input without allocating intermediate Strings. The char-level parser exists for extract_field which predates the navigate API. Both are needed: extract_field for backward compat + simple callers, extract_path_field for the vault backend which needs the zero-allocation path. Unifying to either one would either lose the zero-copy property or require significant API breakage.
